seoTitle: How to Add a Video Overlay in Canva Easily
metaDescription: Layer videos with Canva's overlay tool to add branding, call-to-action text, or extra footage on top of your main video content.
intro: Video overlays let you stack additional footage, text, or branding on top of your main clip, which is a common way to make marketing and social videos more engaging. This guide covers accessing and customizing the overlay tool in Canva. It's useful for marketers, content creators, and educators who want their videos to carry more visual information without extra editing software.

steps.heading: Access the overlay tool
steps.body: Open your video project in Canva and find the option to add an overlay layer.

steps.heading: Customize your overlay
steps.body: Adjust the position, size, and timing of the overlay so it complements the main footage.

steps.heading: Apply effective overlay elements
steps.body: Add logos, text, or secondary clips depending on what your video needs to communicate.

steps.heading: Finalize and export
steps.body: Review the layered video, then export it in the format you need for your platform.

faq.question: Can I overlay text and video together in Canva?
faq.answer: Yes, you can layer text elements on top of video clips, which is common for calls-to-action or branding.

faq.question: Will adding an overlay slow down my video export?
faq.answer: Export times can increase slightly with more layers, but it shouldn't be significant for typical short-form videos.

faq.question: Is video overlay available on Canva's free plan?
faq.answer: Basic overlay functionality is generally accessible on the free plan, though some premium video elements may require Canva Pro.
